Govern.description: Establish and monitor cybersecurity risk management strategy, expectations, and policy

GV.OC.name: Organizational Context
GV.OC.evidence: Municipal risk register maintained, annual review

GV.RM.name: Risk Management Strategy
GV.RM.evidence: Enterprise risk framework approved by Council

GV.RR.name: Roles, Responsibilities & Authorities
GV.RR.evidence: RACI matrices for all Tier 1 systems

GV.OV.name: Oversight
GV.OV.evidence: Quarterly reporting to Director — monthly cadence proposed

GV.SC.name: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Mgmt
GV.SC.evidence: Vendor assessment program covers top 20 vendors — expansion needed

Identify.description: Understand organizational context and cybersecurity risk to systems, assets, and data

ID.AM.name: Asset Management
ID.AM.evidence: CMDB covers 85% of IT assets — OT asset inventory in progress

ID.IM.name: Improvement
ID.IM.evidence: Lessons learned tracked but improvement metrics not formalized

Protect.description: Use safeguards to manage cybersecurity risks

PR.AA.name: Identity Mgmt, Auth & Access Control
PR.AA.evidence: Azure AD + MFA + Conditional Access + PAM

PR.AT.name: Awareness & Training
PR.AT.evidence: Quarterly phishing sims, annual security training, new-hire module

PR.DS.name: Data Security
PR.DS.evidence: Encryption at rest for databases — DLP partially deployed

PR.PS.name: Platform Security
PR.PS.evidence: CIS benchmarks applied to 70% of servers — hardening gaps on legacy

PR.IR.name: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
PR.IR.evidence: DR plans for Tier 1 — Tier 2/3 plans need updates

Detect.description: Find and analyze possible cybersecurity attacks and compromises

DE.AE.name: Adverse Event Analysis
DE.AE.evidence: SOC triage process, threat intelligence feeds, alert correlation

Respond.description: Take action regarding detected cybersecurity incidents

RS.MA.name: Incident Management
RS.MA.evidence: IR plan current, tested quarterly, RACI defined

RS.AN.name: Incident Analysis
RS.AN.evidence: Forensic capability, root cause analysis process

RS.CO.name: Incident Reporting & Communication
RS.CO.evidence: Internal comms defined — external (IPC, provincial) needs formalization

Recover.description: Restore assets and operations affected by cybersecurity incidents

RC.RP.name: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
RC.RP.evidence: DR plans tested for 6 of 8 Tier 1/2 systems

RC.CO.name: Incident Recovery Communication
RC.CO.evidence: Business unit notification process defined — public comms template needed
